entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: Essential plant nutrient whose availability and dynamics are studied in soil systems

entities.name: INDO GANGETIC PLAINS
entities.description: A large fertile plain in North India and Pakistan

entities.name: NORTH-WEST INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A farming system based on minimal soil disturbance and crop residue retention

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of leaving crop residues on the soil surface to improve soil health

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal crop used in the studied cropping systems

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop used in the studied cropping systems

entities.name: MUNGBEAN
entities.description: A legume crop used in the studied cropping systems

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used in the studied cropping systems

entities.name: ROUTINE METHOD
entities.description: The neutral normal ammonium acetate extractable method used to predict potassium supply

entities.name: QUANTITY-INTENSITY APPROACH
entities.description: An approach used to predict potassium supply based on soil solution and solid phase equilibrium

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Traditional farming practices involving tillage and lacking residue retention

entities.name: SUB SURFACE DRIP IRRIGATION
entities.description: An irrigation system that delivers water directly to the root zone

entities.name: AVAILABLE POTASSIUM
entities.description: The fraction of potassium in the soil that is readily available for plant uptake

entities.name: PLANAR POTASSIUM
entities.description: A parameter derived from the quantity-intensity approach to describe K supply

entities.name: LABILE POTASSIUM
entities.description: The readily exchangeable and soluble fraction of soil potassium

entities.name: POTENTIAL BUFFERING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of the soil to maintain the potassium concentration in the soil solution

entities.name: EQUILIBRIUM CONCENTRATION RATIO
entities.description: A parameter (CRe0K) used in the quantity-intensity approach to assess K availability

entities.name: SOIL K DEPLETION
entities.description: The process of potassium reduction in the soil over time
